core_mechanism: construct-validity critique of a proxy indicator, showing that a widely-adopted measurement encodes the biases of the very social/political process that generated it rather than the latent quantity it claims to capture, so treating the proxy as ground truth reflexively reproduces the phenomenon it purports to measure (a circular / self-legitimating measurement)
brief_reason: The abstract's method is not mathematical but argumentative: it audits MBFC as a measurement instrument, showing it is one person's opinions presented as authoritative fact and is 'a computationally legible account of hegemony' that quantifies the outputs of political processes, so using it as neutral data reproduces the bias misinformation research aims to detect.
